note: TWO views recorded. `execution_plan_static` is verbatim from camelid /api/capabilities for the loaded Qwen3-4B-Q4_K_M model. `runtime_observed` is what actually executed (directly measured). They DISAGREE on the decode backend: the static planner fails closed to a CPU label for the unrecognized Q4_K_M quant, but the resident GPU engine actually drove decode. The disagreement is a disclosure-labeling gap (follow-up), not a correctness issue — generated tokens are identical to llama.cpp.

reasons: non-validated row or quant; failing closed to safe path

evidence: serve/bench-generate log: '[gpu] all 36 layers resident in VRAM (5122 MiB free) — full GPU speed'
coherent greedy output identical to llama.cpp at 1/5/50 tokens (see parity json)
CPU path (CUDA hidden) ERRORS with 'no-row-major-data ... data_len=0' on blk.0.attn_q.weight because K-quant tensors load wire-only — so the working run cannot have been on CPU

followups: Make the execution-plan classifier recognize Q4_K/Q6_K dense resident models and label decode_path/selected_backend as the kquant cuda resident lane (currently mislabeled cpu_reference/safe_cpu_decode).
Display name 'Qwen3 4B Instruct Awq' is a cosmetic name-inference quirk for file_type 15 (Q4_K_M); the quant is Q4_K_M, confirmed by bench-generate quantization field and gguf tensor-type histogram.
